name: Rich Cocoa Fudge
recipeInstructions:
1. Line an 8-inch or 9-inch square pan with foil and coat the foil with butter.
2. In a heavy 4-quart saucepan, combine sugar, cocoa, and salt.
3. Stir in milk and cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until the mixture reaches a full rolling boil.
4. Continue boiling without stirring until the syrup forms a soft ball when dropped into cold water (234°F or bulb of candy thermometer rests on the side of the pan).
5. Remove from heat. Add butter and vanilla. Do not stir.
6. Cool at room temperature to 110°F (lukewarm).
7. Beat with a wooden spoon until the fudge thickens and loses some gloss.
8. Spread quickly into the prepared pan and cool.
9. Cut into squares. About 36 pieces or 1-3/4 pounds.
Variations:
Nutty Rich Cocoa Fudge: Beat cooked fudge as directed. Immediately stir in 1 cup chopped almonds, pecans, or walnuts and spread quickly into the prepared pan.
Marshmallow-Nut Cocoa Fudge: Increase cocoa to 3/4 cup. Cook fudge as directed. Add 1 cup marshmallow creme with butter and vanilla. Do not stir. Cool to 110°F (lukewarm). Beat for 8 minutes. Stir in 1 cup chopped nuts and pour into the prepared pan. Fudge will not set until poured into the pan.
High Altitude Directions:
Increase milk to 1-2/3 cups.
Use soft ball cold water test for doneness or test with thermometer in boiling water, subtract the difference from 212°F. Subtract that number from 234°F. This is the soft ball temperature for your altitude and thermometer.
